655	
656				res, err := action.Execute(&velero.RestoreItemActionExecuteInput{
657					Item:           &unstructured.Unstructured{Object: unstructuredSvc},
658					ItemFromBackup: &unstructured.Unstructured{Object: unstructuredSvc},
659					Restore:        test.restore,
660				})
661	
662				if assert.Equal(t, test.expectedErr, err != nil) && !test.expectedErr {
663					var svc corev1api.Service
664					require.NoError(t, runtime.DefaultUnstructuredConverter.FromUnstructured(res.UpdatedItem.UnstructuredContent(), &svc))
665	
666					assert.Equal(t, test.expectedRes, svc)

17	package actions
18	
19	import (
20		"encoding/json"
21		"testing"
22	
23		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
24		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
25		corev1api "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
26		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"

29

31		"github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/pkg/builder"

33		velerotest "github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/pkg/test"
34	)
35	
36	func svcJSON(ports ...corev1api.ServicePort) string {
37		svc := corev1api.Service{
38			Spec: corev1api.ServiceSpec{
39				HealthCheckNodePort: 8080,
40				Ports:               ports,
41			},
42		}
43	
44		data, err := json.Marshal(svc)
45		if err != nil {
46			panic(err)
47		}
48	
49		return string(data)
50	}
51	
52	func svcJSONFromUnstructured(ports ...map[string]any) string {
53		svc := map[string]any{
54			"spec": map[string]any{
55				"ports": ports,
56			},
57		}
58	
59		data, err := json.Marshal(svc)
60		if err != nil {
61			panic(err)
62		}
63	
64		return string(data)
65	}
66	
67	func TestServiceActionExecute(t *testing.T) {
68		tests := []struct {
69			name        string
70			obj         corev1api.Service
71			restore     *api.Restore
72			expectedErr bool
73			expectedRes corev1api.Service
74		}{
75			{
76				name: "clusterIP/clusterIPs should be deleted from spec",
77				obj: corev1api.Service{
78					ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
79						Name: "svc-1",
80					},

content.text: I found the failing path in `pkg/restore/actions/service_action.go`: `deleteHealthCheckNodePort` uses `NestedFloat64`, which errors when the last-applied JSON field exists with a `null` value. Next I’m adding a focused temporary regression case that captures the expected behavior.

input.description: Run focused service action tests after cleanup

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `pkg/restore/actions/service_action.go`.

Summary:
- Replaced `unstructured.NestedFloat64` for `spec.healthCheckNodePort` in the last-applied annotation with `unstructured.NestedFieldNoCopy`.
- `null` values are now treated like “not explicitly specified,” so Velero clears auto-assigned health check node ports instead of failing.
- Numeric and string non-zero values are still treated as explicit ports and preserved, matching the intended behavior.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test reproducing `"healthCheckNodePort": null`.
- Confirmed it passed with the fix.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Re-ran focused tests after cleanup:

```bash
go test ./pkg/restore/actions -run TestServiceActionExecute
```

Result:

```text
ok  	github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/pkg/restore/actions	0.059s
```

No commit was created.

- `null` values are now treated like “not explicitly specified,” so Velero clears auto-assigned health check node ports instead of failing.
- Numeric and string non-zero values are still treated as explicit ports and preserved, matching the intended behavior.

Validation:
